pages.metaDescription: Providing Oklahomans across the state with fresh Oklahoma grown bermuda and fescue sod. Delivery and pickup available.
headings: All sod is harvested fresh to order.
Visit us on Facebook for weekly updates on our sod and other information!
Ranks #1 In the Following Categories In NTEP & University Testing
keyText: Skip to content Sod By Sherry® Inc.
Growing turf 40 years and still rolling OKLAHOMA SOD FARMOrder Sod Now All sod is harvested fresh to order.
We partnered with Tahoma 31 on several major projects, including supplying the Kansas City Chiefs training facility in June 2024.
That same year, we contributed to the Top of the Rock golf course renovation and the 3Bird golf training facility.
The WinStar project is officially featured in Golf Course Architecture magazine.
The story is now live in their digital issue and is also highlighted as a Featured Project on the Tahoma 31 website.
It’s a strong showcase of the work that went into WinStar Golf Club and a great opportunity to share the success across social media and other platforms.
We supply our sand-based Tahoma 31 to The “Palace on the Prairie”, Owen Field, home of OU Sooner football!
Visit us on Facebook for weekly updates on our sod and other information!
Cale Bigelow, a professor of turf science and ecology at Purdue University, located in West Lafayette, Indiana, participated in the NTEP study.
In a December 2019 article in Golf Course Management magazine, Dr.
Bigelow was quoted as saying only 9 of 42 bermudagrasses tested survived with at least 50% ground cover by spring.
Some 14 of the grasses completely died.
By the end of the NTEP study, Tahoma 31® was rated by far with the greatest winter survivability in Indiana at only 4% winter kill.
Mean scores in two states where winter survivability was measured, Indiana & Kentucky, confirmed Tahoma 31’s top status in the three-year study period, 2014 – 2017.
Overall, Tahoma 31 used 18% less water than TifTuf.
#1 in Traffic Tolerance Tahoma 31® exhibits excellent traffic tolerance compared to all other bermudagrass cultivars tested.
Checking Order Status To check on your order status due to weather delays, please email your order number to sodbysherry@gmail.com.

pages.title: Sprigging
keyText: Skip to content Sod By Sherry® Inc.

Sprigging Certified Tahoma31 NO-TILL Fairway or Ball field Renovation by the numbers We have seen Tahoma 31 show promise in choking out and taking over sub-standard Bermuda varieties with it’s thick density and ultra-short internodes (growth points).

The full floatation tires on our new 5125R John Deere, coupled with our new 7 ft Sprigger’s choice fairway Sprigger has 3 sets of independent press wheels to plant on curves and contours while maintaining proper planting depth in hard conditions.

5 day schedule of events Day 1 Spray the turf areas to be planted to Tahom31 with Primo 4X rate at 44 oz per acre, this will stall the existing turf for 3 weeks to give an early grow in window for the new Tahoma31 to take root.
Day 3 Scalp the existing turf to ¼” by means of either a rotary mower, flail mower, or fraze mower.
The areas should stay scalped for 3-4 weeks to help the Tahoma31 take root.
Day 4 Triple aerify the field on 4” centers with ½” or 5/8” cores.
This will help loosen up the existing turf offering a better planting bed for the Tahoma31 to be planted in.

Day 5 Sprig the fairway or ballfield 2 directions on a 30-degree angle at a pass rate of 600 ga/bu per acre totaling 1,200 ga/bu per acre, this high planting rate will add insurance to the success of the project and speed up the turf conversion.

After the second pass, set the irrigation system to water 5 min per zone on the areas that were planted, then we will pass 4 times with our Culti-Crimper to press more growth points into the new field surface.
Then turn the water on to water 15 min every 2 hours for 3 weeks.
Golf or sports play can resume on the 3-week mark.
Tahoma 31 should come up evenly and begin to take the field over, this full conversion can be expected over the course of the next 3-5 years.
17000 West Foreman RoadSodBySherry@gmail.com(405) 354-7926 Powered by WordPress | Theme: Astrid by aThemes.

pages.title: Installation
keyText: Skip to content Sod By Sherry® Inc.
Installation Every load of turf is delivered to your jobs cut-to-order fresh with plenty of dirt on the sod.
We bring the turf to your job on a just-in-time basis and take away what is leftover, charging for exactly what we use.
Our employees take great pride in every installation.
The consistent handling of the turf from our fields to your job results in a stellar turf product and the highest quality service.
Our installation services include light raking, cut-to-fit installing techniques, and power-rolling with a 1,200 lb.
roller after we lay the grass to ensure proper soil to sod contact.
This eliminates air pockets to the roots that could cause dead spots.
Because we grow all of our own grass, we can control every aspect of Total Quality Management in sod production.
We will never dig any grass unless we have an order.
We offer several options for our grass service: We load the grass for you at our field closest to you, we need a day notice to have it dug fresh.
We deliver the grass to your location and spot the pallets around the job at your discretion.
We usually need a week’s notice on delivery depending on the time of year.
We come to your location, provided the ground is final graded, and install the grass at your discretion.
With this service, we arrive with your fresh cut order of grass, install the sod, and roll it with a 1,200 lb.
power roller to ensure a level finished yard.
Power rolling also ensures good root to soil contact to eliminate air pockets that may damage the grasses.
We staple slopes when needed on steep inclines.

pages.title: History
keyText: Skip to content Sod By Sherry® Inc.
History Sod by Sherry® was established in 1983 by my late grandfather; Virdin Royse, my father; Dan Sherry, and myself, Brad Sherry.
Dan left the FAA in 1980 to start Sherry Enterprises Inc., which specialized in mowing and lawn maintenance of several housing additions in the Oklahoma City area.
Through the years, this business required occasional sod purchases.
The quality of sod purchased was not as good as the jobs required.
Dan Sherry partnered with the late Oklahoman cattleman Virdin Royse to start Sod by Sherry.
Preliminary ideas about sod production arose.
First, ideas about how many acres of cattle land the sod farm would consume.
Second, how was Sod by Sherry® going to differentiate its product to compete in the established sod market effectively?
Third, ideas arose about how our farm could grow and produce a quality turf product.
Looking back we see that through controlling every aspect of sod production including; growing, digging, installing, and power rolling, our sod farm has been able to become a Total Quality sod farm.

Through the years, our third-generation family business has been successful through strict quality management, technological innovations, and a strict “cut to order” policy to ensure the Total Quality of our grass.
We believe that our grasses and the attention to detail in our workmanship are better than Oklahoma has experienced in the past.
It’s a growing field; somebody has to do it.
We are still doing it because of our strict Total Quality Management, and excellence in all areas of operations.
Look at our first advertisement in 1985 for sod, back in the good old days when a Coke cost $0.35 cents!!
17000 West Foreman RoadSodBySherry@gmail.com(405) 354-7926 Powered by WordPress | Theme: Astrid by aThemes.

pages.title: Tahoma31® Care
keyText: Skip to content Sod By Sherry® Inc.
Tahoma31® Care Sod By Sherry®, specializing in growing and developing high-performance turf varieties, acclimated to our climate.
Since 1983 we have been at the forefront of the turfgrass industry in Oklahoma.
All sod is harvested as orders come in so we don’t just let the grass sit around and dry out while waiting for a buyer.
This ensures the customer a fresh product on every order.
We now accept Visa, Mastercard, American Express, and Discover credit cards at our sod store for retail pallet pickups and retail deliveries.
We do not accept credit cards on wholesale orders.
Figure your square footage by taking your length times width.
Late Winter (January 20-February 10th) when the grass is still dormant, Spray 80 oz.
per acre of oxidization sc pre-emergent mixed with a quart of 2-4-D with good surfactant and 40 gal clean (not hard water) water per acre.
Regular 2-4-D applications may be necessary if broadleaf weeds arise, or MSMA is fine to use if“grassy weeds” arise in the summer.
May 1st, Begin mowing at your desired height with a reel or rotary type mower.
Keeping your blades sharp and catching the clippings would be ideal.
Tahoma31® looks the best when kept at a 1″ height of cut.
Fertilizer schedule using Scott’s Turfbuilder program or equivalent April 1st Starter fertilizer May 1st Southern fertilizer June 1st Southern fertilizer Sept 1st Fall Lawn Food Nov.

1st Winter guard Watering, since Tahoma 31 has been proven to require much less water to maintain color and quality than other varieties, we see only extended periods between rains are to be a concern for watering.
Ideally, an irrigation system set to water twice a week with a week “rainfast delay” would be the best situation.
After the installation of Tahoma 31®, it is important to water three times a day for two weeks.

pages.title: Sod Care
headings: Spring and Summer guidelines for sod care or new Bermuda sod installed green.
Fertilizer schedule using Scott’s Turfbuilder program or equivalent
Fall and winter guidelines for sod care for new Bermuda sod installed in dormancy (not green)
Guidelines for newly installed Fescue sod
keyText: Skip to content Sod By Sherry® Inc.
Sod Care Spring and Summer guidelines for sod care or new Bermuda sod installed green.
KEEP IT GREEN: Upon installation, water immediately.
For two weeks after, water 15 minutes twice every midday, and 20 minutes every evening.
The grass is established when it can’t be pulled up from the ground.
Then continue to water 20 minutes a day.
SETTLING HAPPENS: When new structures are built on idle land, many ground disturbances must occur, such as burying of sewer, electric, and sprinkler lines.
Ample sod watering provokes the land to settle around these disturbances.
Lawn settling is not the fault of the builder, developer, or sod installer; it is a common consequence of modern construction.

TOP DRESSING: The only way to regain a level yard after settling: Wait three weeks after the grass is established Buy some sand or light fill dirt Using a shovel, cast the sand into the depressed areas of the yard Use the backside of a rake to level the piles.
The grass will spread across and grow through the sand within six to eight weeks, depending on the thickness of the sand.
FERTILIZING: Our sod is fertilized in the field every month of the growing season.
Upon establishment, fertilization maintains grass health, produces a deep green color, and promotes root depth.
Use Scotts Southern Turfbuilder or an equivalent blend of “10-20-10” every month during the growing season.
Water is critical during the post-fertilization time: Usually, 30 minutes a day for a week should produce a lush green lawn.
Fertilizer schedule using Scott’s Turfbuilder program or equivalent April 1st Starter fertilizer May 1st Southern fertilizer June 1st Southern fertilizer Sept 1st Fall Lawn Food Nov.
1st Winter guard Fall and winter guidelines for sod care for new Bermuda sod installed in dormancy (not green) DORMANT, NOT INVINCIBLE: Sod is a perishable product even when installed in dormancy.
It must be cared for properly to survive the cold winter months.

SKU: tahoma31sprig Category: Sod Tags: bermuda, sod, tahoma Description Additional information Reviews (0) Description Fine textured and beautiful, Tahoma 31® Bermudagrass is well suited for use on golf courses, sports fields, and lawns.
It is noticeably the softest turf you will ever put your bare foot on.
It topped the charts in handling 63% shade compared to other improved varieties.
It has proven to use 50% less water than standard varieties.
It is best kept mowed 1 1/2″ or less, standard rotary mowers are fine but it can be reel mowed down to 120 which is putting green height.
Developed by Oklahoma State University.
Related Additional information Size Half Pallet 250 sqft, Full Pallet 500 sqft Reviews There are no reviews yet.

SKU: tahoma31 Category: Sod Tags: bermuda, sod, tahoma Description Additional information Reviews (5) Description Tahoma 31® Bermudagrass is known for both its improved cold-hardiness and improved drought resistance.
Tahoma 31® was bred from two cold-hardy, drought-resistant plants found in some of the most extreme landscapes on the planet.
The Mother Plant was collected on a hike in the mountains on the east side of the Himalayas in China, a region known to be very cold and arid.
The Father Plant, a fine-textured grass, was collected a world away in South Africa, also known for its cold, dry climate.
Much greener than the other grass in the yard.

The only noticeable difference would be seen during the transition times of the year, this turf stays green a few weeks longer and greens up a few weeks earlier than standard U-3 Bermuda.
